item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: After woman's murder, detectives learn killer was "only half the story"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered in broad daylight in Carrollton, Texas, Andrew Beard, the father of her child, became a suspect. Investigators would eventually discover a twisted murder plot they say was orchestrated by his fiancée, Holly Elkins.

item.title: Texas mom's killer received text before murder: "I hope you handle it"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered, detectives quickly learned that the prime suspect was the father of her child, Andrew Beard. But as the investigation unfolded, they would find out that Beard wasn't the only one involved in the murder plot.

item.title: The Plot to Eliminate Alyssa Burkett
item.description: A toxic couple orchestrates an elaborate plan to kill a mother. "48 Hours" correspondent Peter Van Sant reports.

item.title: At least 12 people wounded in shooting near festival in Toledo, Ohio
item.description: Police in Toledo, Ohio, reported that there were believed to be at least two shooters. No suspects have been arrested.

item.title: Ex-boyfriend and fiancée behind fatal stabbing, shooting of Texas mom
item.description: What appeared to be an open-and-shut case for Texas investigators turned out to be a twisted murder plot involving victim Alyssa Beard's ex-boyfriend Andrew Beard and his fiancée Holly Elkins – who detectives say was the mastermind.

item.title: Toledo police give update on shooting near festival that injured at least 12
item.description: At least 12 people were wounded in a shooting near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio, officials said Saturday. The Toledo Police Department gave a press briefing on the incident.

item.title: Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort stokes days of protests in Albania
item.description: The so-called "Flamingo Revolution" has taken up the cause of protecting the Albanian coast from a development led by the president's son-in-law.

item.title: Missing Auburn University student in Japan found dead, mother says
item.description: James Higginbotham was found dead in a mountainous area outside Kyoto by a volunteer search-and-rescue group, his mother said.

item.title: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire as stalemate continues in peace talks
item.description: The U.S. military reported that it has shot down six Iranian one-way attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz, while seven ballistic missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain were largely intercepted as well.

item.title: Pratt's lead over Raman further erodes in new L.A. mayoral race results
item.description: Political newcomer Spencer Pratt's lead over Los Angeles City Councilwoman Nithya Raman in the race for L.A. mayor has continued to diminish since election night.

item.title: Golden Tempo wins the Belmont Stakes after also taking the Kentucky Derby
item.description: Golden Tempo made Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train a Kentucky Derby winner and the second woman to train a Belmont Stakes winner.

item.title: Meet the horses that ran in the 2026 Belmont Stakes
item.description: The Belmont Stakes hosted a New York rematch of the top two finishing horses from the Kentucky Derby to wrap up horse racing's Triple Crown for 2026.

item.title: How fans are feeling ahead of the World Cup
item.description: Seattle's Space Needle sported a new look on Saturday, painted like a soccer ball. The city is one of 16 across North America set to host men's World Cup games. Nicole Valdes reports on how die-hard fans are getting ready.

item.title: Insulin inhaler could become alternative for Type 1 diabetes insulin injections
item.description: A medical breakthrough is showing promise for millions of Americans with Type 1 diabetes. It's an alternative to taking insulin without the injections. Mark Strassmann has more details.

item.title: Protests in Albania over a Trump family luxury resort project
item.description: Saturday marked the seventh day of protests over a planned luxury resort in Albania linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump. Activists call it the "Flamingo Revolution." They've adopted the pink bird as a symbol of the wildlife they say will be destroyed if the billion-dollar project goes ahead. Ian Lee has more.

item.title: Body of missing Alabama student found in Japan, his mother says
item.description: The mother of 20-year-old missing Auburn University student James Weston Higginbotham posted on Facebook that a group of search and rescue volunteers had found her son's body in a mountainous area outside Kyoto, Japan. Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances in California governor's race as he awaits runoff challenger
item.description: Democrat Xavier Becerra has advanced to the general election for California governor, CBS News projects. As of Saturday, it is still unclear who Becerra will face, as ballots are still being counted. Kelsi Thorud reports from San Francisco.

item.title: Gas prices drop slightly 14 weeks into Iran war
item.description: Trump visited rural Wisconsin on Friday, telling farmers an economic rebound is coming. Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: Middle East sees most intense exchange of fire between U.S., Iran since ceasefire started
item.description: The fragile ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. faced new strain on Saturday following the latest spasm of violence. Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Dangerous storms across multiple U.S. states
item.description: Severe weather is ripping through multiple U.S. states like Virginia, Pennsylvania and Louisiana. Andrew Kozak has the forecast.

item.title: 6/6: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Dangerous storms threaten multiple states; Iran accuses U.S. of violating the ceasefire as both sides exchange strikes.

item.title: Iran soccer team departs for Mexico ahead of World Cup
item.description: Problems with processing visas had earlier led Iran to move its training base from Tucson, Arizona, to Tijuana, Mexico.

item.title: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men. Here's why
item.description: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men in search of quick cash and thrills, experts say. "I had almost $4,600 at one point but squandered that," one man said.

item.title: Veteran runs to fight "life-changing disease" after minor symptoms
item.description: Dr. Sara Whittingham thought she would know if something was wrong. But her minor symptoms had a surprising cause.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties: What to expect when Trump attends NBA Finals
item.description: As President Trump prepares to watch the New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden, officials are planning for a heightened security posture, sources said.

item.title: Actor James Handy allegedly killed by girlfriend's son at LA home
item.description: Michael Gledhill, 44, was arrested on suspicion of murder after he turned himself in following the fatal stabbing of Handy, the LAPD said.

item.title: Why a 55-mile trek is a mandatory rite of passage for freshmen at one high school
item.description: The five-day, 55-mile Appalachian Trail hike is a 53-year tradition for freshmen at St. Benedict's Preparatory School.

item.title: Officer told captain of flight that hit light pole he was "a little low," NTSB says
item.description: The NTSB released its preliminary report on a United Airlines plane that struck a light pole on the New Jersey Turnpike in May.

item.title: Ned Jarrett, NASCAR Hall of Famer and CBS announcer, dies at 93
item.description: Ned Jarrett was inducted into the NASCAR Hall of Fame in 2011 after 50 career wins on the sport's top circuit.
